name: Dakota Bread
recipeIngredient: 1 Egg white, frothed with fork (for glaze)
recipeInstructions:
1. Stir yeast and honey into water; let stand until foamy, about 5 minutes.
2. In a food processor fitted with a metal blade or mixer with dough hook, combine remaining bread ingredients in a bowl. Turn the machine on and combine the mixture.
3. With the machine running, slowly add the yeast mixture. Mix until the dough cleans the sides of the bowl. If the dough is too sticky, add more flour by the tablespoon, working it in before adding more. If the dough is crumbly and dry, add more water by the teaspoon, working it in before adding more.
4. Once the desired consistency is reached (moist but not sticky), mix the dough until well kneaded, uniformly supple and elastic, about 40 seconds in the food processor, or about 6 minutes in the mixer. Alternatively, hand-knead the ingredients in a large bowl; work well.
5. Make a well in the center of the dough. Pour the yeast mixture into the well. Work the yeast mixture into the ingredients, then knead on a floured board until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes.
6. Transfer the dough to a large plastic bag, remove all air, and seal the top. Place the dough in a bowl. Let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1 1/2 hours.
7. Oil a heat-proof glass pie plate. Sprinkle lightly with cornmeal.
8. Punch down the dough and shape into a smooth ball. Place the smooth side up in the prepared plate. Cover loosely with oiled plastic wrap. Let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1 hour.
9. Fifteen minutes before baking, put the rack in the center of the oven; set the oven to 375F (190C).
10. When the loaf has doubled, brush the top with egg white. Sprinkle with wheat germ and/or rolled oats.
11. Bake until the loaf is deep brown and sounds hollow when tapped on the bottom (cover with foil last 5 minutes if it's too dark), about 35 minutes.
12. Place the loaf on a rack to cool.
Yield: 1 round loaf
